sermons.title: Simplicity in Devotion
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: At midweek service on wednesday, we received the ministry of Apostle @pst_iren as he taught oh the topic “The Ironic Disadvantages of Excellence” ✨ He highlighted that there are things we experience that changes the quality and expectation of your life forever. At a foundational level in every aspect of our lives, excellence is a very good thing. 📍 What do we do when our excellence or things we regard as excellence is subconsciously affecting our devotion and we don’t see it? Here are key points to note: ➔ When we come to a point where our prayer life is powered by electricity and prayer aids, and we cannot pray, we must realize the change. ➔ Worship is getting costly. We don’t realise that our service programming is compensating for our distracted lifestyle. ➔ If we don’t master the boredom of consistent devotion, we will never be happy with our walk with God. ➔ Due to our constant use of worship aids, we have distracted ourselves from the voice of God. There is too much going on to hear him. 🔥A hack for devotion: Find a way to reset and come back to the normal setting of simplicity. Many times, Revival is the restoration of simplicity. How to Restore Simplicity to Your Devotional Life. ➔ The Discipline of Repetition. If a sermon really blessed you, stay there. Dont listen to another one yet. Re-listen for a good number of times before you move. Repetition is not the lack of words, it is the key to persuasion. One of the proof that you are touching God is simplicity of your devotion. Take note of the people who think their walk with God is too mature for daily devotion. They struggle with consistency. When simple things bless you, you’re growing. Embrace the boredom of consistency. ➔ By Gratitude. Sophisticated people are sometimes ungrateful and competitive; always looking for the next thing and not fully enjoying at God has done. When you’re grateful, there are many things people gloss over that you stay on and heaven always remembers. A culture of gratitude preserves simplicity. ➔ By Staying. You will miss many notable things in your life, if you don’t know how to sit with God for hours. The most important quality of spirit that helps you pick information from the other side is stillness. If you don’t know how to be still, your life is under attack. ➔ Make God the Focus. As long as he remains the focus your life remains simple and at the bottom like. When you look at others, you lose gratitude. Keep Christ as the focus. ➔ Be a Doer. Don’t just acquire knowledge, be a doer. ➔ In worship, focus on substance. Make sure the song lyrics make sense and that’s what you’re responding to. Heaven will always pay attention to the revelation of God’s word and Christ is the revelation of God.

sermons.title: Grace On Monday
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: 📌 Work has always been God's idea from the very beginning. In Genesis 2:8, God planted a garden, and Adam continued what God started. This shows that work was always an extension of worship, not a punishment or distraction from it. ✨ When your motives are right, showing up diligently in your job, business, or craft is pleasing to God. 📌 God wants us to engage the world, not hide from it. He wants us to dominate, build, create, and shape culture. We were reminded that we are not built for the church alone, we're built to shine our light in secular spaces too! 📌 Just like the prophets in the Bible days, who had both spiritual and administrative excellence, God is raising believers who can pray in tongues and manage systems. God wants those who can host His presence and drive innovation. By giving us a means of livelihood, God has given us power to get wealth (Deuteronomy 8:18) 📌 Divine provision is not manna falling from heaven; it's God opening doors, giving you ideas, and trusting you to work with His wisdom. Because Grace doesn't cancel work, Grace empowers it. 🔥 ✨ True worship continues beyond Sunday. It's seen in how we live, how we work, and how we represent Christ daily. ✨ Grace gives us the strength to build, create, and influence the world around us for His glory.

sermons.title: Nations & Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: There are four non-negotiable marks of a good church: - A good understanding of salvation and the gospel. - Their theology about money. - They must have a charismatic ministry in a corporate way - It must be a mission-minded church.   Here are other key points to note: - Every message you hear in church should encourage you to engage in the things of the Spirit. - You have a part to play in the kingdom. Tell people about Jesus at every chance you get.

sermons.title: Special MDWK Service - Praying In Tongues III
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The gift of tongues is not the exuberance of a particular denomination, it is hinged to the very promise of our salvation. 📌Luke 24:25-27; You get into a new level of Bible study when you understand that the priority of the scriptures is salvation. The entirety of the scripture has a scope. 📌It begins with the promise of salvation in the Old Testament, the provision of salvation in the Gospels, and the announcement of salvation in the Epistles. 📌So, there are three Jewish feasts that God instituted through Moses that talk about Jesus’ coming: 1. The Passover: This was a feast to commemorate God delivering the people from Egypt. - This was a very powerful metaphor of salvation. The children of Israel were not going to be saved just because they were children of Israel. The only thing that would occasion their exemption from death was the blood on the lintel. This means that the only reason the Egyptians died is because they didn’t know. If they had known, they’d have put blood on their lintel. This is a picture of the final judgment. It doesn’t matter your background, the only thing that will save you at the end is the blood. - During the Passover, God asked them to eat unleavened bread. From I Corinthians 5:7, we understand that the unleavened bread was to represent sanctification because the yeast was a metaphor for people who didn’t believe. 2. The Feast of Weeks: Immediately after the Passover celebration, they begin to count 49 days after the Passover. 49 days is 7 weeks; Exodus 34:22. The feasts are connected. There is no feast of weeks without the Passover. - According to God’s timeline, in delivering the Israelites from Egypt, they should have been in the promised land by 50 days and partaken of the first fruit of that land. - In the Passover, they ate unleavened bread, but by the feast of weeks, they bake 2 loaves of leavened bread to say ‘We who used to be slaves are now free’ - The feast of weeks was supposed to commemorate the giving of the law. 3. The Feast of Tabernacle By studying these feasts you ought to have understood God’s redemptive plan and His timeline. 1. Christ is our Passover: I Corinthians 5:7; so the unleavened bread had nothing to do with eating, it related to people. What happened in Egypt was a prophetic picture. - The Passover was celebrated on the 10th day of the first month - Every Jewish family will buy a lamb, familiarize themselves with the lamb for three days, and clean their house of every leavened bread. - On the 14th day, they’ll kill the lamb without breaking its bones. 📌When we come to the Gospels, we see that on the 10th day of the first month, Jesus gets a donkey and rides into Jerusalem. This means that the lambs the families were buying were a type and shadow of the real Lamb. He is then brought before Caiphas, the one that handles all the temple sacrifice. He is then sacrificed at the same time that the lambs are being sacrificed. 2. Christ is our first fruit: I Corinthians 15:20-23. The Jews were also called first fruit because through them, the world would hear about Christ 📌And so, Pentecost is attached to salvation. Tongues is not the exuberance of a particular denomination, it is hinged to the very promise of our salvation. 📌Now, what does this have to do with tongues? 📌God showed the Israelites so many signs so they could understand that He was behind their prophets. So for God to catch their attention He must do it in a dramatic way. He must show that Jesus is greater in a more dramatic way. The mount of transfiguration. (2 Cor. 3:6) At the giving of the law, 3000 people were judged and killed but at Pentecost, 3000 people were saved. 📌If God did all that in the Old Testament, we must have even more signs. Fire will fall not on Sinai, but on us. The God of the Bible will always announce himself.

sermons.title: Special Miracle Service - Praying In Tongues II
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: God uses foolish things in the world to confound the wise, and it is the same with the gift of tongues. Here are some key points to note from the sermon: - It takes spiritual discernment to recognize the operation of faith through speaking in tongues. - Tongues are the trigger to every other spiritual gift - Tongues is not determined by how eloquent it sounds. What does it mean to speak in tongues? - The gift of tongues is a unique spiritual language given to you. - It is a spiritual communication between you and God. - It is for the believer to use for edification in spite of it being unintelligible to the mind. What does it mean to speak in tongues? - The gift of tongues is a unique spiritual language given to you. - It is a spiritual communication between you and God. - It is for the believer to use for edification in spite of it being unintelligible to the mind. What the Tongues gift is not? - It does not mean to speak in a human language you never learned; this is only possible by a miracle. - Tongues are not for evangelical purposes. - Speaking in tongues is not involuntary. How do I receive the gift of tongues? - Recognize that God is willing to give you, more than you are ready to receive it. - It is impossible to ask God for the gift and not receive it. - If you are born again you already have the spirit, you only need to use what you have.

sermons.title: Edifice - Praying In Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Praying in Tongues.🔥🔥🔥 The gift of tongues is important for every believer. Therefore, it must be properly explained for better understanding. Here are some key points to note from the sermon: - God doesn’t want you to be ignorant about tongues. - As long as you have God’s spirit, you can speak in tongues. - To fully understand this gift, you must look into the word of God to know what God says about it. Why is Tongues important? - When we speak in tongues we confirm that Christ is glorified. There is no other spiritual gift that directly points to the glorification of Christ. - We have access to it only because of Christ’s resurrection. - It is God’s desire that we are not lacking in any spiritual gift. So, do not resist the urge to flow in this gift of tongues. The Holy Ghost is for everyone!🔥

sermons.title: Paper Tiger
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Minister: Apostle Emmanuel Iren Title : Paper Tiger Objectives of this sermon -To highlight the origin of Fear -To understand what God done about Fear -To understand how to handle Fear 📝Where does fear come from? Fear is seen in the Bible as early as Genesis 3:10 where Adam and Eve were afraid after they disobeyed and ate of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil. Tree of the Knowledge of Good and Evil is a principle of carnal perspective of life. The devil (serpent) convinced them by telling them: -Their eyes will be open - Genesis 3:4 i.e. they will have carnal illumination, giving them a new perspective of wisdom - Genesis 3:6 -They will be as God, meaning they would not be subject to the law of God anymore Romans 8:7 However, after they ate of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, their eyes opened and they felt ashamed and anxious (Genesis 3:10)- this is the origin of Fear. 📝The solution to Anxiety is not that your needs are met. James 4v1-3 When anxiety arises, some believe that fulfilling specific needs will bring happiness. While milestones like job, marriage, success are good, they should not be dependent on your happiness. 📝What has God done about Fear: Philippians 4:6-7: Anxiety might have been normalized but it is not normal, it’s a foreign attribute. God has given you his spirit - Romans 8:2. You are free from the law of sin and death. Luke 12:25 - Worry is pointless, it won’t make you better. You must learn to handle things you can’t control, God wants you to practice peace. Philippians 4:6-7 - When you are hard pressed on every side, take everything to God in prayer, and the peace of God which surpasses all understanding will guard your heart, which means God responds to not just your prayer but also gives you peace. 📝How do you produce this peace? With prayer and supplication. Prayer is therapeutic. Spend time praying in tongues. God’s priority in spite of all that you’re going through, is not just to answer you, he wants to build your spirit and make you strong because in this world, you’ll have tribulation.

sermons.title: Brighter
sermons.speaker: Pst. Laju Iren
sermons.description: Brighter ✨ In 2 kings 6:13-16, Elisha and his servant were in the same situation but they saw things differently. Elisha’s servant was afraid of what he could physically see while Elisha was not afraid because of his spiritual enlightenment. Your level of light is what determines your level of victory. Why do we need light? ✨Light in the word will keep you in difficult times ✨The Man in Christ is at an advantage when it comes to the word of God- 1 Corinthians 2:9-10. ✨The word of God cannot be difficult to understand because you have the Holy Spirit- 1 Corinthians 2:14 Points to Note 📝 Faith in Jesus Christ is the first step to understanding scriptures - Ephesians 1:15 📝There are dimensions of light and you determine your dimension Three dimensions of light ( The Parable of the Sower) 1. Easy reception with no roots ( Mathew 13:20-21) 2. Reception but wrong environment (Mathew 13:22) 3. Bearers of fruits (Mathew 13:23) ✨How to grow in light 1. Study the word of God- Ephesians 3:3-5 2. Meditate- Joshua 1:8 ( points on meditation: (a)focus (b) Water the word of God by praying in tongues (c) Memorize scriptures (d) Repeat ( Go again) 3. Get a good teacher of the word 4. Run with word ( obey the last command) Jeremiah 8:9. Learn to obey God’s command. Doing what God will have you do is as important as knowing what God will have you do.

sermons.title: RC 22 - Contagious - Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Why bother to pray? 1. For God's sake You might not know what you are saying, but it does not change the fact that he hears. God is listening, you are not doing it for activity's sake. (1 Cor 14:2, Heb 12:2, Psalm 42:1) 2. For People's sake. You must see how your prayer might be what the person beside you needs. Maybe someone in your family needs it, for the body of Christ. 3. For your own sake There is no time of prayer that leaves you the same way. When you spend time praying, you avert the plan of the devil and strengthen yourself for what is ahead. Your physical strength might deplete, but your spiritual strength is built. (Isa 40:29-31) Despite all it is for, it is labour. It will take something from you. So; 📍You must persevere. Luke 18:1 📍You will practice fervour. 📍You will leverage koinonia. Take advantage of the community beside you. Heb 10:24. 📍You will be goal-oriented. Like Hannah, like Jesus. 📍You will engage your mind and your heart. Keep your mind focused on the goal

sermons.title: Octane - How To Pray - The 5C's of Prayer
sermons.speaker: Pst. Laju Iren
sermons.description: Preacher: Pastor Laju Iren Sermon: The 5C’s of Prayer. Wrapping up the prayer series for the month of September, Pastor Laju taught the 6C’s (Yes. She added an extra and vital C😉) of prayer. Now, it is important to note that for our prayers to be effective, maintaining a prayer culture is vital and the points below will equip you for this task.🧏🏼‍♀️ 🔸 Consistency (1 Thessalonians 5:17, Romans 12:12, Ephesians 6:18) Daily conversations with God are so important as it can only be called a prayer culture when it is consistent. 🔸 Celebration (Ephesians 1:15-16,Acts 19:20, 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18) Never let the devil talk you out of thanksgiving over what God has done for you in the past. If you are conscious of the goodness of God, you will pray more because a culture of celebration will lead to a culture of prayer. 🔸 Conviction The guilt of not praying enough is one thing that stops people from praying. So, there must be a conviction of who God is and who you are in him. A conviction of who you are praying to and the fact that he hears you. 🔸Concentration It is important to pay attention as you will get more results in prayer if you concentrate. You can also practice praying in your understanding intermittently just as you pray in tongues. 🔸Confidence (Luke 8:51-52) There are miracles you will not see except you are brave enough to pray in spite of contradiction. 🔸 Company (Hebrews 12:22-23, 11 Kings 6:15) When we pray together, things happen. Prayer is a weapon and praying together is a strategy. Another glorious thing we should be conscious of is that we have spiritual company. A whole army of Angels at our beck and call. Glory to God.🔥

sermons.title: Octane - A Call to Prayer
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Octane - A Call to Prayer. Why should we pray? •Prayer can birth a prophetic season •Prayer aligns you with the will of God •Praying in tongues makes you extraordinary •As a believer, your future is guaranteed through When you are conscious that God is your loving Father, you can pray with confidence. The Holy Spirit is our guarantee that God hears us when we pray.

sermons.title: APV - Akwa Ibom - Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: APV - Akwa Ibom - Tongues

sermons.title: Praying In Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Matthew 20:26 God loves to brag about people that contribute. If you want to stand out in the sight of God you should be ready to contribute. Medals are awarded to those who fight not to those who know that they can fight because in the kingdom God doesn’t reckon greatness by capacity alone. At the end of the day, greatness in the kingdom is not by what you think you can produce but by what you actually produce. Greatness is reckoned by contribution! What really measures greatness in the sight of God is “Love” (1 Cor 13) Even in the administration of gifts, there is a hierarchy in terms of importance, know this, Spiritual gifts are for service! It is not so that you can advance your own physical agenda. God wants a body whereby every part is a supplying joint and If you’re asked what you’re doing for the body of Christ; It should be seen that you’ve been able to come up with a strategy to reach people for God. (1 Cor 14:1-2) Acts 1:8 simply means that the gifts are primarily for evangelism and not for personal gratification. Write your faith story! There has to be more. Desire spiritual gifts but not for yourself. The Spirit of God has come to make greatness common. Get involved for the kingdom and be a supplying joint. Again, desire the gifts for the advancement of the kingdom! 1 Sam 10:5-7 God designed his church to be a body filled with supplying joints. If you really want to see big things in your walk with God, associate with people who have it. 1 Sam 10:7 explains that we can do as occasion demands once the spirit has come upon us. Do not let the devil trick you to think that being alone is the best thing. Take community seriously and use community as a strategy. When our heart is focused on the kingdom we have the capacity but we must have a purpose in mind and God uses utterance to launch us into our purpose. By this, we experience progress and joy in the faith!

sermons.title: Soli Deo Gloria - Global Day 3 - Morning - Why Tongues?
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: God wants us to abound not just in knowledge, but in utterance also. Tongues is a gift from God. When we pray in tongues, we confirm the testimony of Christ. Tongues is a spiritual gift that proves that Christ is glorified; no other gift points to this. If God has made this gift available, then we must use it. In the things of the spirit, there is no discrimination. The gift of tongues is for everyone. Not speaking in tongues is a departure from God's plan. Below are three reasons why you should pray in tongues: 1. Why not? 1 Cor 14:2 makes it clear that he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries. 2. When we speak in tongues, we prove that Christ is glorified. 1 Cor 1:5-7 3. When we pray in tongues, we edify ourselves. Jude 1:20 Be alert whenever you pray in tongues because there is always something to see and hear!

sermons.title: Deeper - Praying By The Spirit II - Glosso Lalia
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: PRAYING BY THE SPIRIT - GLOSSO LALIA. Faith is our Norm! (Luke 7:9) Some people don’t believe In God because they didn’t retain their capacity. Anybody whose heart doesn’t pant after God has done something wrong. Christ does not make people hungry Acts 10:20 The Samaritan woman, John 4:20 Christ gives to the thirsty and He makes sure that you both meet halfway in the journey. Spiritual impact is not all about the efficacy of the word. The first question is what is the condition of your heart. Great faith does not tolerate excuses, I must be among and have a later type of curiosity. Be ready to thrive (1 Cor 1:4-7) covet earnestly for the gifts, occupy until he comes. Of all the reasons you should pray in the spirit, the testimony of Christ has to be confirmed in you. Speaking in tongues is the confirmation of the testimony of Christ. It shows indeed Christ is glorified. Acts 2: 32 Tongues are a mystery and it takes revelation to understand. 1 Cor 14:2. Barbaric tongues is proof that you’re getting IT. God will pour out his blessings and gifts to all men. Joel 2:28; Eph 5:18. In conclusion, Tongues is communicating words of utterance that only make sense by the spirit. The problem is not the supply, it all starts with your approach. Come and drink!

sermons.title: Apostolos - ABJ Day 2 - True Impact - Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Your perspective of the Church must correspond to the way God designed it; view it through his lenses and approach it the Bible way. (Matt 16:18, Mk 16:15-17) Let your heart embrace God's provision and design; His plan & design is that everyone in the Church participates in charismatic ministry. The church of Jesus Christ is a tongue-speaking church. Decide not to have it any other way. (1 Cor 1:1-6) Speaking in tongues is a confirmation of the testimony of Christ. Every other miracle existed before the Redemptive work except speaking in tongues, it is only possible because Christ is glorified. (Act 2:32-33, Act 2:4)   3 things to know about tongues 1. It can be controlled. (1 Cor 14:32) 2. It is not done in human languages. (Act 2:17, 1Cor 14:2,6-19) If tongues were done in human languages, there would be no need for the gift of interpretation of tongues. 3. We can all do it together. (1Cor 14:27) Speaking in tongues is the best anti-depressant. Speak in tongues more. Use tongues to your advantage, it is for your spirit, body, and your mind. Give your spirit expression through tongues. To function more in revelation gift, pray more often in the spirit.

sermons.title: The New Normal - Prophecy
sermons.speaker: Pst. Iren Emmanuel
sermons.description: Eph 5:18 And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit; Eph 5:19 Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord; He puts them side by side, he was suggesting that there's a way to get high without damaging your body. Spiritual Gifts have a fabric; a way they are meant to function. Paul was telling us to seek edification. A gift is as important as the needs that need to be met. He was emphasizing the importance of the gift of tongues and interpretations. Tongues and interpretations = Prophecy

sermons.title: Final Authority of the Scripture
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Here are a few key points from the teaching: 📌 The Word is our standard The Word of God serves as our guide and reference point in every area of life (Psalm 119:105). 📌 A burden for the body of Christ: As believers, we must be concerned about the suffering of Christians around the world. * The commonwealth of the Kingdom calls us into unity. * We are called to care for and stand with one another (1 Corinthians 12:25–26) 📌 Identity above passions Our desires should not define us: * Our identity is rooted in Christ (Galatians 2:20) * The Gospel remains the ultimate authority in faith and practice. Nothing contrary to Scripture should be binding on a believer * The Word stands as our final authority (Isaiah 8:20) * Culture must not override truth (Romans 12:2) 📌 The danger of weak convictions: Without strong convictions, believers can easily be swayed. * We must remain grounded in truth (Ephesians 4:14) 📌 Truth with compassion We are called to reflect both truth and love: * Show compassion, just as Jesus did (Matthew 9:36) * While remaining firm in the truth of the Gospel. 📌 Confidence in the Word The Word of God remains living and powerful, able to stand the test of time.

sermons.title: The Working of The Spirit
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The Holy Spirit is given to every believer to guide, teach, and empower for godly living. The Spirit reveals the mind of God and brings understanding of spiritual truths. He works in the heart to produce fruit such as love, joy, peace, patience, and self-control. The Spirit empowers believers for service, ministry, and witness. Prayer is effective when led by the Spirit, who intercedes beyond human weakness. Walking in the Spirit means yielding daily to His leading rather than following the flesh. Spiritual gifts are distributed by the Spirit to build up the body of Christ. Sensitivity to the Spirit’s voice requires obedience, humility, and a pure heart. The Spirit convicts of sin, assures of salvation, and strengthens faith. The Christian life is victorious only through dependence on the Holy Spirit.

sermons.title: Fueling The Engine
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: FUELING THE ENGINE”. We were reminded that Church is not an event, but a living organism to which we belong (1 Corinthians 12:12, 21). Here are a few key points from the service: 1️⃣ Many of us think of “church” as a place we go to or a program we watch. But in God’s design, the Church is a body, every part connected, every member needed. 2️⃣ Just like we feel the pain of a toothache when one small part of the body is hurt, the absence or silence of one member affects the whole. Our presence, voice, and contribution matter, not just to people, but to God Himself (Numbers 11:27, Ephesians 4:16). 3️⃣ The early Church understood this. They weren’t spectators; they were participants. Just like in Numbers 11:27, where even those who did not gather still received the Spirit, God has marked us as part of His family. 4️⃣ Belonging comes with responsibility. Ephesians 4:16 says that every joint supplies. We must be dependable for our contributions to the corporate mission, not as silent spectators, but as active participants. Apostle also showed us, from Scripture, what a Biblical Church really looks like, and how it differs from today’s common idea of church. This brings us to the acronym ICAME: 📌I – IDENTITY Church is not an event; it is a family and a people. We have an identity. 📌C – COMMITMENT Not as customers, but as covenant members. We don’t serve God as we like but as He likes. The cross demands our honor and reverence. 📌A – ACCOUNTABILITY Every church has leaders, and God expects us to submit, receive corrections, and walk in love. Paul even says in 1 Corinthians 5:4-5 that to be outside the local church is to be in Satan’s territory. 📌M – MISSION Our lives must align with the corporate mission of the Gospel, not just personal pursuits. 📌E – ECONOMICS Families share responsibility. In the same way, God expects us to steward His house with generosity. As 1 Corinthians 9:7-11 reminds us, no soldier goes to war at his own expense. Out of love and duty, we ought to give consistently to fuel the spread of the Gospel. As we continue this week, let us do the following 👇🏾 ✅ Be intentional about showing up for our local church gatherings. ✅ Look for ways to serve with our gifts. ✅ Practice cheerful, consistent giving for the mission. ✅ Embrace accountability and spiritual discipline. ✅ Live each day on gospel mission. The train is moving! The Gospel is advancing, and God has called us to be part of His engine. 🚂

sermons.title: RC 23 - Grand Finale
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Our Apostle Iren exhorted us in the word of God and it was so instructive.🔥 - Be committed to the place of fellowship with the saints. Do not give up at the slightest inconvenience. - Christianity isn’t only about what you were delivered from, it’s also what you are delivered into. - You’ve were adopted into God’s family - When choosing a church, choose with the conviction of the Spirit. - The books in the New Testament were to churches and not lone Christians. - Evangelism gives us the opportunity to grow in spiritual gifts

sermons.title: Deeper Abuja - Wisdom - Discerning Spiritual Seasons
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: How to identify ‘Negative Kairos’ 📍Matthew 16:1-3; just as there are natural signs of physical seasons, such is the same for the spiritual. 📍Kairos can be described as a divine coincidence, but it's more than that. You might never even realize when it happens. It's God's prophetic intervention in human activities. 📍Luke 10:30-31. Synkarian- together with Kairos, which is now translated to coincidence. 📍Isaiah 46:9-11. God can declare the end from the beginning and announce it like it has already happened. With God, favour can come from a far place. Kairos works with the gift of prophecy. 📍There is a negative kairos. It's possible for positive and negative Kairos to happen at the same time for different people in the same situation. 1 chronicles 18. 📍Negative kairos can happen either by you straying away from God and being under the devil's control or by someone else prophesying negatively over you.

sermons.title: Deeper Uyo - Noon - Sin & Holiness
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Sin & Holiness (Afternoon Session) Apostle Emmanuel Iren There is a difference between what is Fundamentally Right and what is supernaturally right. The Bible describes Sin in two words - Katah and Armatia. Both of them mean: to miss the mark. To be Holy means to be separate. Holiness describes excellence. Holiness describes sacredness. Holiness is not Moral Excellence. If Moral excellence is holiness how about when the Bible uses an inanimate object as holy like the mountain? 📌 Covetousness Covetousness is a Sin. Ephesians 5:3, Colossians 3:5 We are people devoid of greed as believers. Covetousness in another context, God said you should do it. The Bible says to covet earnestly the best gifts, and covet spiritual gifts. 1 Corinthians 12:31 Is covetousness right or wrong? There are some things that are wrong if you put them in the wrong context. 📌 Passion There is a type of passion that when directed at God is not wrong but any other thing you love like with that type of passion is a sin. There is a dimension of sin that in and of itself is not wrong, it is just directed to the wrong thing.

sermons.title: Deeper Ago - Morning - Manifesting The Presence of God
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Manifesting The Presence of God Apostle Emmanuel Iren The Bible with regard to the presence of God is context specific. - The Omnipresence of God refers to God being everywhere at once (Psalm 139:7-8NKJV) - The omnipresence of God keeps us accountable - The omnipresence of God applies to all men and creatures while the indwelling presence of God applies only to those who believe in Jesus (John 14:23) As believers He has made us His temple and dwelling place. Even though God is everywhere at once He doesn’t manifest everywhere. There is a training required to manifest the presence of God. God’s answer to every need of man is the presence. God doesn’t need to break chains. Chains break off on their own in the presence of God. (Acts 12 :7 ) God doesn’t recognize limitations. Jesus went where no one will go in Matt 8:28 There is a level of authority that empowers you to go where no one can go. Learn to be conscious of the manifest presence of God If you have the in dwelling presence of God you can stir up the manifest presence of God. It takes consciousness. You must lose satisfaction with the ordinary life. How to manifest the presence of God ▪️By Knowledge- Have a reorientation of your understanding. This will change your expectations. ▪️Doing God’s work-Spiritual gifts get stronger with use. If you don’t use it you won’t grow in it even if you have it. ▪️Demonstration - You will never see the power that you are not ready to demonstrate. Learn to put power to test with confidence. Don’t play it safe Talk back at the devil. Our words are not cheap.

sermons.title: Special Sunday Service London Afternoon
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Housing Spirits and Spiritual Gifts. Spiritual gifts are our way of describing the diverse ways that the same Spirit can operate. As a believer, you have the Holy Spirit dwelling in you and He can be expressed in different ways. The same Holy Spirit can work through you as the need demands. Don't limit Him! 1Chron 29:2, 4; 2Chron 7:1-2;6:18-20; Acts 7:47-49; Acts 17:24

sermons.title: APV Ibadan - Fire Conference - Praying With Fire
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Praying with Fire God didn't create things to limit himself, but for order. That's why we see a lot of stories in the Bible where things happen outside normal chemistry. Exo 3:1-5 There's a type of fire that doesn't destroy but makes things better. HOW DO YOU STIR UP THE GIFT OF THE SPIRIT 📌Understanding. You have to understand you have the spirit, you have fire! Romans 8:15 Receiving the Holy Spirit changes everything, including prayer. And prayer changes because the relationship has changed. When you understand prayer can be an irresistible cry of a child. The consciousness of the father changes prayer. 📌Trust God. Trust that he always has your best interest at heart and live in it. 📌 Call it! You need to be able to direct it and pull the trigger. Say what you have received. Speak into it! If you want something, you have to pray like you have it and talk like it. You can kindle something from inside it.

sermons.title: Octane - Touch & Agree
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: TOUCH AND AGREE God has given us the Holy Spirit to do so much with but unfortunately, we are doing so little Sometimes we make things difficult for ourselves because we have not learned to use what we have. Develop a godly curiosity to learn about who you are in Christ and identify what is yours so that you can use it and get better. Of all the beautiful things that a smartphone can do, one of the most powerful things is sharing. The true test of Koinonia (fellowship) is your contribution to the Body of Christ. 2 Corinthians 13:14. God designed for the Holy Spirit to be experienced in fellowship. There is a capacity you can never tap into if you try it alone. Another word for Communion (of the Holy Spirit) is “Contribution”. It doesn’t matter how gifted you are, the wisdom of God is manifested where everyone gets to contribute. We must get used to corporate worship, corporate ministering, and corporate fellowship, not only the ministering from the pulpit. Fellowship happens with people who have things in common and because we have the Holy Ghost, we can contribute. The true test of koinonia is contribution. What areas can we contribute? In the word and through the gifts of the spirit Colossians 3:16

sermons.title: The Devotion Code
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: According to basic science, for fire to be produced & sustained, three things must be present: heat, oxygen, and fuel. Remove any, and the fire goes out. The same principle applies to spiritual fire; revival does not happen coincidentally, it occurs as a result of deliberate architecture. If you put the right things in place, you will inevitably see it. There’s no such thing as an ‘automatic Christian life’, there is what to do to sustain what God has done. Fan the flames of the gifts of the Spirit, and fight the good fight of faith. 1 Tim 6:12; Gal 5:15 Jesus exposed the spirituality of many seemingly physical phenomena. When you struggle to pray, it’s not ordinary, there is war. Matt 26:41 There are things you can do to build a consistent devotional life. 👇🏻 Spiritual maturity means growing in the knowledge of Christ and superimposing that knowledge in your daily experience. Acknowledge and manifest the knowledge that you have received. Spiritual maturity is hard work, there is diligence to it. 1Pet 2:9 Your walk with God will enter a new level when you learn to handle your Gethsemane experience - where you fight your flesh. Don’t observe your devotion instead of controlling it. Whether you feel like it or not, pray. Set your desire. A mature Christian does not observe his feelings, he observes his spirit. Col 3:13, 1Sam 10:4-6 The most invaluable key to a sustainable devotional life is fellowship with the Saints. This unites everything; just by proximity, fire spreads. All through the Bible - Spiritual gifts thrived in community. Col 3:2; 1 Sam 10:5; Phil 1:25; You must continue to see who you want to be. It’s in your spirit to replicate your destiny; when you consistently see someone doing what you desire, you replicate it. Don’t keep your mind empty; play prayer tapes, sermons, and spiritual songs. Music is a very powerful tool in the atmosphere of the Spirit. Acts 16:25 For it to be a New Testament Church, the workings of the Spirit must be common. You have the Holy Ghost so you have something to offer. Eph 5:1

sermons.title: Sponsored By The Spirit
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: God did not give you his spirit for nothing. The spirit of God in your life must count for more than goosebumps. Zechariah 4:6 He gave you the Spirit because the assignment he gave you cannot be accomplished by your power. Do something extraordinary You might be timid but there’s something called the zeal of the Lord. God the Father is glorified in the glorification of the spirit. John 14:12 Now we are partakers of the commonwealth of Israel. In the New Testament, God has made greatness common. You need to understand what is yours by the Spirit; 1 Cor 12:1 the display of Spiritual gifts doesn’t have to be once in a while it’s something you can master and can be replicable. Paul is emphasizing that you can function in all the spiritual gifts. Functioning in the word of wisdom and knowledge is called supernatural leadership.

sermons.title: Praying In Tongues
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Matthew 20:26 God loves to brag about people that contribute. If you want to stand out in the sight of God you should be ready to contribute. Medals are awarded to those who fight not to those who know that they can fight because in the kingdom God doesn’t reckon greatness by capacity alone. At the end of the day, greatness in the kingdom is not by what you think you can produce but by what you actually produce. Greatness is reckoned by contribution! What really measures greatness in the sight of God is “Love” (1 Cor 13) Even in the administration of gifts, there is a hierarchy in terms of importance, know this, Spiritual gifts are for service! It is not so that you can advance your own physical agenda. God wants a body whereby every part is a supplying joint and If you’re asked what you’re doing for the body of Christ; It should be seen that you’ve been able to come up with a strategy to reach people for God. (1 Cor 14:1-2) Acts 1:8 simply means that the gifts are primarily for evangelism and not for personal gratification. Write your faith story! There has to be more. Desire spiritual gifts but not for yourself. The Spirit of God has come to make greatness common. Get involved for the kingdom and be a supplying joint. Again, desire the gifts for the advancement of the kingdom! 1 Sam 10:5-7 God designed his church to be a body filled with supplying joints. If you really want to see big things in your walk with God, associate with people who have it. 1 Sam 10:7 explains that we can do as occasion demands once the spirit has come upon us. Do not let the devil trick you to think that being alone is the best thing. Take community seriously and use community as a strategy. When our heart is focused on the kingdom we have the capacity but we must have a purpose in mind and God uses utterance to launch us into our purpose. By this, we experience progress and joy in the faith!

sermons.title: The Supply Of The Spirit For Healing
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: From Mark 16:17, we know that every believer is capable of performing signs and miracles. We cannot afford to be unaware or ignorant about it. (1 Cor 12:1 ) Operating in the spirit is compulsory not just for you but for the sake of the people around you. Healing can be an event but it can also be an environment. Some sicknesses are not merely a medical condition. Some are spiritual Eg in Matt 9: 32-33 God wants us to cultivate a healing atmosphere so we can walk in divine health. In Exodus 4:17, Moses’ rod wasn’t given to him by God. It was his. But by that same rod, Moses was able to perform signs. In the same way, by the spirit of God within you, you can perform signs and miracles. You can do all things by the Holy Ghost inside of you. Glory!

sermons.title: Go
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: GO! Jesus was a carpenter, yet He was doing "fishing consultancy" for people who were professional fishermen. When they caught so much fish that their fishing net tore. It is expected that they go to the market to sell all the fish. However, there was something much more than that. Jesus wanted to make them FISHERS OF MEN. Matthew 28:18-19 1. This is a call to maturity.Jesus is saying unto you, "Come up hither". 2. You can and should get better at spiritual things. In Christianity, there is nothing like a "better yesterday". *Spiritual maturity and growth is not for some set of people. It is for EVERY single Christian. 2 Corinthians 3:6. *Are you scared of teaching and preaching? You can LEARN. You can get better at spiritual things. 3. Whatever you are good at bears witness to the fact that you can also be good at evangelism. The early apostles did not just wake up and start doing miracles, they were under training and did "internships" when Jesus was alive. Luke 10:1   CONVERSATION STARTERS WHEN YOU WANT TO PREACH THE GOSPEL. A. Indirect Approach-John 4:3 Go to people and make friends with them. Lay hands on the sick. Have conversations with strangers and start dispensing the gifts of the spirit. B. Direct Strategy There are people you meet and you immediately tell them about Christ. This is because you might never see them again and this might be their last opportunity to hear the gospel.   1. Who do you believe is the greatest person in history? 2. Have you lost anyone close to you? Where do think dead people go? 3. Do you know any good church around? 4. What do you think when people talk about Jesus? 5. I am praying for people today, what do you want me to tell God about you?   Two things to note as you GO! 1. Get Godfidence. Stand firm, be unshakable and unmovable. Matthew 9:5 2. You are not as shy as you think. Rehearse how to evangelize. Sin makes people VICTIMS. The solution and way out is JESUS. By preaching the Gospel, we are giving them the antidote.

sermons.title: Compelling Power
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: You'll only experience what you earnestly pursue and desire. Your desire will be evident in the steps and actions you take. So desire spiritual gifts and never stop! It's also important not to adjust your theology to accommodate your unfavorable experiences. We can learn from the woman with the issue of blood, even after 12 years she still had faith that she'll be healed if she touched the hem of Jesus' garment. Remember, God wants involvement in the miraculous for everyone like in the early church so desire compelling power and pursue it!

sermons.title: Deeper Abuja - The Man God Uses (Kingdom Priesthood)
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: THE MAN GOD USES; A Teaching on Biblical Priesthood. 1. In the realm of the spirit, sacrifice is the picture of strength, & service is the picture of greatness. Rev 5:1-10 2. The Gospel is not just a message to believe, it's an example to emulate; Christ sacrificed his life to save us; don't just benefit from this provision, imbibe the mentality. Phil 2:4-5 3. The mentality of sonship is different from the mentality of the priesthood. Priesthood (service) is the purpose of sonship (adoption); after adoption comes priesthood. 1 Pet 2:9, Rom 9:1-4 4. The church of Jesus must have a depth of maturity, each person having a sense of divine direction as a royal priesthood. 5. You don't trap the goodness of God in selfishness, when you reflect on God's goodness, it should provoke selflessness in you. For example, the salvation plan should make you want to live your life for Jesus. Lk 5:9-11, John 6:51 6. Spiritual gifts have a purpose, they are meant for service, not for self-preservation. Two (2) things that make spiritual gifts ineffective; a.) Lack of love. b.) Wrong motive. 1Cor 13:1-5 Desire spiritual gifts NOT for your self-preservation, but so that you can serve the church, this is how to be effective. 7. Priesthood is a step deeper. A priest has the responsibility to represent God to the people and the people, to God; there is selflessness to it. Have this mentality in your daily devotional life. Your selfless is revealed in your motives. Deut 18:1-2, Exo 29:1-4, 15-18, 19-28, Rom 12:1 8. A better approach to Christian experience is this; 'Christ died for me so that I can live my life for him. If you seek God first so that other things can be added to you, you are still seeking other things. Matt 6:33 9. Bear the burdens of God. Grow up. You are a priest, stop thinking about yourself & what you can gather. The man God reveals & entrusts his plans to is a selfless man. 10. Pray selflessly, e.g for the spread of the Gospel, for the freedom of the persecuted. Develop genuine compassion for others. Grow in obedience, not in religion. 2Thes 3:1-2, Heb 13:3, Eph 1:7

sermons.title: Vintage Jesus - Baptizo
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: BAPTIZO! In the Old Testament types, shadows and symbols were used to foretell the redemptive work of Christ. This is the concept of Typology For eg White lambs were used to typify the True Lamb who will be spotless. Circumcision was used to typify the cutting off of the sinful part of man in Salvation. Baptism - to illustrate the burial and resurrection of Christ as a redemptive work. (Romans 6:3 -4) Communication in the Old Testament is a key difference between the New Testament In the Old Testament, there were types, shadows, and symbols, replete with the prophetic. (Heb 1:1 -3) The prophets said nothing wrong but the Son of God has more authority and clarity. In Christ, you can’t get the revelation of God wrong. In Christ, you see the exact image of God Heb 10:1 3 ways to identify a figure 1. It is made with human hands. Any aspect that involves man’s effort doesn’t reflect the redemptive reality in Christ. 2. If it predates the New Testament, it is not an ordinance in Christ and therefore shouldn’t continue. Some things can predate and should still continue but there must be a balance. It should tick the other boxes. 3. If the New Testament clearly identifies it as a symbol. In 1 Cor 5:7 Christ is our Passover. In Colossians 2:11 Christ is our circumcision Baptizo - Greek word of baptism (to Immerse.) 1 Cor 12: 13 Baptism is done by the Spirit and not with human hands By baptism, we get spiritually added to the lineage of Christ Colossians 2:12 describes how to be a beneficiary of what Christ did There are salvation metaphors Eg poverty Hunger and thirst Just like water, salvation satisfies and cleanses. In John 7:37-39 Jesus referred to water here as the spirit Christ mentions water repeatedly as a symbol of the Spirit in John 3:1-5 Water baptism = a symbol representing baptism by the Spirit. When the same thought is passed across all the synoptic gospels, we must know its significance. The baptism of Christ is of the Holy Spirit! (John 1:32-33, Luke 3:16, Matt 3:11, Mark 1:8) The baptism of water was only the picture of salvation. A type and shadow of what we now have by the Spirit! (Acts 11: 14-17)

sermons.title: Prophetic Faith
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Prophetic Faith When you look at the Eagles, many of the things people admire about them are natural tendencies for them. They do not 'strive' to soar, they simple do because it is their nature. That is the same way it should be with Christians. There are so many things that are already built-in us because we are in Christ, but yet we struggle with them. If we follow the fundamentals of faith like it's taught, we see some people who don't follow these fundamentals and yet they get a completely different result. Why then is the miraculous relatively easy for some people who don't seem to be following the 'principles' of faith. To understand God's mind concerning any subject at all, it is important that you pick every verse concerning the subject and interpret it as one. In Matthew 4:6, the devil quoted the scriptures to tempt Jesus and although the scripture was true, in that context, the motive was wrong. Faith has a context and faith has a motive. Mark 11:24 and James 4:3 shows that there should be a balance in the context and motive of Faith. Philippians 4:13 has been misinterpreted without understanding the context. The preceding verse explains the context for which that verse stands. The aim of faith is not self-gratification but God's glorification. What then is Faith? * Faith is the desire for the will of God, who has your best interest. * Faith is moving in God's direction and easing of heart. * Faith is not combating the will of God, but moving with God's passionate plan. Hebrews 11:33-40 * Faith is also the gift of the Spirit. There is a God-initiated Faith, where even when you are on your own, the Spirit is supplying Faith for a purpose. * Faith is both a gift of the Spirit and the fruit of the Spirit. The business of faith is to incline yourself to the heartbeat of God and following His pulse. There are things that God pursues with such fervency, and so if you align yourself with that you will walk in the prophetic faith. Loss reveals the purity of your faith. If it is faith, it is replaceable.

sermons.title: Apostolos - LAG Day 2 - Corporate Anointing
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The Corporate Anointing. Acts 17: 28 - tells us that we are God's offsprings. God does not depend on us humans for anything. Neither does the Lord does not dwell in temples made with hands (Acts 7:57). We cannot build a temple for the God who created the heavens and earth. (2 Chron 6:18-20) By the Holy Spirit, Now we have become the address of God. We are God's dwelling place (Gal 3:5, Eph 2: 21). Together we are temples and individually, we are temples. The beautiful things in the old testament were a foretaste of our actual reality. The capacity of what we carry within is way bigger than that in the world. We don't learn for learning's sake, we learn for ministry (Eph 4: 11) and we contribute because we are joined by the spirit (Eph 4: 16). We have a huge role to play and the body of Christ will not grow until we play our parts. Until we realize how the body of Christ is meant to function, we will live below potential. Elijah was a man like us. If we pray as Elijah did, we would get even better results. The experiences of the people of the old testament should challenge us. (James 5:17) Each one of us has individual ministries. We all have the power to manifest in the Spirit at the same time. This is corporate participation. 1 Chron 14: 26 Discernment comes before actions. We have the ability to do both and more. You will never know you have something until you use it. The gifts of the spirit have been given for ministry. If you do not do ministry, your walk with God will suffer. A principle to thriving spiritually; Find someone to disciple. Romans 1: 11(NKJV). In every part of the Bible where believers prayed together, there were extraordinary results. Don't let your grades dwell dormant. Utilize every opportunity to pray with believers. Acts 4, 6 The power that rose Jesus from the dead, resides in us. What will you do with it?

sermons.title: Desiring Spiritual Gifts
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Spiritual Gifts are not for a select few; every believer can work effortlessly in them.

sermons.title: The New Normal - Prophecy
sermons.speaker: Pst. Iren Emmanuel
sermons.description: Eph 5:18 And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit; Eph 5:19 Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord; He puts them side by side, he was suggesting that there's a way to get high without damaging your body. Spiritual Gifts have a fabric; a way they are meant to function. Paul was telling us to seek edification. A gift is as important as the needs that need to be met. He was emphasizing the importance of the gift of tongues and interpretations. Tongues and interpretations = Prophecy

sermons.title: SENT; Learning to Obey
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Apostle showed us through scripture that even Jesus “walked in obedience through the things He suffered,” revealing that obedience and character are developed through real-life situations, not theory (Hebrews 5:8–9). A thought-provoking question was raised: Why did God place Adam in a garden that contained the tree of the knowledge of good and evil? In response to this Apostle taught that there is a difference between what God knows and what God wills. We learned that God knew what could happen, but His will was for man to take dominion (Genesis 1:26). Here are key truths we learnt from the sermon: 📍 Dominion requires something to dominate. Adam’s assignment in the garden involved exercising obedience even in the presence of the serpent. 📍 True dominion includes discernment. It involves knowing what to do and what not to do, what to touch and what not to touch. 📍 Dominion cannot be learned theoretically. Dominion is practiced in real situations. Without challenges or choices, there is nothing to exercise authority over (Genesis 1:26; Psalm 91:13; Luke 10:19). 📍 Character is trained, not gifted. It is developed over time, not instantly downloaded. Growth happens through obedience and discipline (Hebrews 5:8–9). 📍 Your identity may be known, but your character will still be tested. Even though the enemy knew who Jesus was, he still tested Him (Luke 4:3). The same pattern appears in the lives of God’s people (John 1:14; Job 1:10). 📍 Character should be valued above skill. Scripture places strong emphasis on character over ability (1 Corinthians 13:1; 1 Timothy 3:2). 📍 Without character, success becomes a trap. When promotion comes before maturity, it can become dangerous rather than beneficial (1 Timothy 3:6). 📍 Self-control is a mark of strength. A person without restraint is like a city without walls—open to destruction (Proverbs 28:25). 📍 Obedience requires adopting the mind of Christ. Believers are called to develop the same posture of humility and obedience that Christ demonstrated (Philippians 2:5). 📍 Faithfulness often shows up in difficult moments. Like the three Hebrew men who refused to bow despite the consequences, obedience means honoring God even under pressure (Daniel 3:16–18). Obedience is not proven in comfort but in moments of testing. Through every challenge, God shapes our character so that our dominion reflects His will. As we go into this week, let us prioritize character development and obedience to God’s Word. When our character grows, our influence and authority grow with it.

sermons.title: The Fear Of God
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Apostle taught on the topic “The Fear of God.” 🔥 He began by emphasizing that the process of growth is as important personally as it is to God. He further explained that salt without seasoning power is no different from white sand. The moment it loses its power of preservation, taste, or flavor, it becomes just white sand. (Matthew 5:13) Here are key takeaways to note: 📍 As a believer, we lose our saltiness when we become identical to what we are supposed to preserve and correct. 📍 We must learn to stand for the truth and never compromise on values that are against our faith. 📍 The scripture outlines that our faith must be reflected in our personal lives. Therefore, our lives must preach what we believe. (Philemon 1:6) 📍 We must ask ourselves - What aspect of our lives shows evidence of our walk with God? 📍As a Christian, we should communicate our faith through our lives, and it must be effectual. 📍 Let people be able to tell from the way we act, talk or dress - the fruits must be evident. Two school of thoughts to note: (Philemon 1:6) 1. Fruits of the Spirit These reflect what is within us internally but seen externally by people as evidence that we are truly children of God. 2. Accountability If we belong to the Kingdom, we should be held accountable to the standards of our confession. 📍 As believers, we cannot be part of the Kingdom and live however we like. The people around us must be able to acknowledge our walk with Christ. 📍 Our total conviction is not tied to just believing in God alone, because even demons believe in God. 📍Faith is the requirement for salvation, but if not evident, it is fake. 📍 The fruit of the Spirit is the evidence that shows a believer. (Romans 10:9) Fear in this context is not the fear of constant punishment, but a continual reverence for God. It is the fear of God that delivers us from every other thing. Some corresponding actions that show fear for God: * Our reverence - recognizing that His fear alone is consuming. * Our decision to stand for Him until the end of your life. * Performing our duties as believers to keep His commandments. * Not living as though we are beyond God or accountability. * Living a life that preaches what we believe through integrity, speech, and conduct. Family, as we have learnt this week and beyond, intentionally live and practice the fear of God. Maintain your saltiness and let your faith be reflected in your personal life. 🔥 What part of the sermon resonated with you the most, and what will you begin to put into action from today?

sermons.title: The Working of The Spirit
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The Holy Spirit is given to every believer to guide, teach, and empower for godly living. The Spirit reveals the mind of God and brings understanding of spiritual truths. He works in the heart to produce fruit such as love, joy, peace, patience, and self-control. The Spirit empowers believers for service, ministry, and witness. Prayer is effective when led by the Spirit, who intercedes beyond human weakness. Walking in the Spirit means yielding daily to His leading rather than following the flesh. Spiritual gifts are distributed by the Spirit to build up the body of Christ. Sensitivity to the Spirit’s voice requires obedience, humility, and a pure heart. The Spirit convicts of sin, assures of salvation, and strengthens faith. The Christian life is victorious only through dependence on the Holy Spirit.

sermons.title: RC 23 - The Ambience Of God in Salvation
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The Ambience of God in Salvation. Here are a few key points from the sermon: 📍 The fruit of the Spirit is the evidence of the influence of the Spirit in a person. (Galatians 5:22 NKJV) 📍 When you observe a person, you can see either of these two things evident in their life: 1. The Fruits of the Spirit 2. The Works of the Flesh 📍 If you don’t show signs of the fruit of the Spirit, it is either because you are not saved or you haven’t submitted to the influence of the Spirit. 📍 Salvation is God fulfilling His redemptive promise to man. 📍 Righteousness is first a gift before an action. When you believe that Christ died for you, you are declared righteous. God has given us the gift of righteousness and power. So, walk in the Spirit and submit yourself to the influence of God.

sermons.title: How To Receive The Word
sermons.speaker: Pst. Laju Iren
sermons.description: How to receive the Word. How do we receive the word? We receive the word: - By honouring the word of God above all else. - Acknowledging the word of God as the final authority. - By receiving the word of God with humility and meekness. - By showing interest and paying attention to details. - By receiving the word of God with Joy, If God says it, it is as good as done. - By receiving the word of God with faith and expectation. The best way to not be blown by the wind of the doctrine is to be established in the truth.

sermons.title: Cinderella - Love, Fairytales, Reality
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: For a relationship to work, it takes more than prayer, fasting and even knowing the right person. How to make your relationship/marriage work👇 ❤️Approach relationship/marriage like a novice - buy books, and get counsel to equip yourself. Be willing to learn. ❤️Never underestimate the amount of preparation required. You must be prepared before getting into a relationship/marriage. ❤️Love is important and also a good starter, however, love/romance isn’t enough. The Bible says in Eph 5:31-32 “Marriage is a mystery.” However, Never call simple what God has called a mystery. A good marriage is hard work but it is possible. The love Christ has for the church makes marriage a total ball game for a believer. Therefore you must do the work and prepare well for a good marriage. How can I be deliberate and put in the work when it comes to my relationship/ marriage? ❤️ Do it as unto the Lord - this is how you can demonstrate the Lordship of Jesus in your life. Your primary performance should not be dependent on the other person’s character but unto the Lord. Ephesians 5v25 - love your spouse as Christ loves the Church. ❤️ Walk in the Spirit - Do not relegate your marriage to sense knowledge. Love isn’t an emotion, it’s a fruit of the spirit. When you love Christ way and any challenge arises with your spouse, it should be an opportunity to demonstrate the love of Christ in his/her life. ❤️ Be intentional - You must love the way the person perceives love. Learn your spouse’s love languages. Know how they want to be loved and treated. Messages for Men: ❤️ Consciously love your spouse- You can do this by thanking her for the things she does. Don’t respond to ‘SERVICE’ with ‘ENTITLEMENT’. Learn to always say ‘Thank you,' make it a habit ❤️ Compliment her always, even in front of other people and your children. ❤️ Validate her dreams & don’t trivialize her ideas

sermons.title: CCI AGO Inaugral Service - Church Mind
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: One of the most emotional texts in the scriptures is John 21:15-17 for these reasons: 1. This was the first time Jesus spoke to Peter after he betrayed him. 2. Jesus had just risen from the dead. Jesus wanted Peter to prove his love for Him by caring for his sheep. If Jesus is indeed the love of our life, we must see that the church is a big deal to him. Hence we can establish two things from John 21:15-17 1. Corporate worship * Church is a group of reverent, obedient, and committed people gathering together regularly. * As believers, what we have in common must bring us together regularly. * We can't claim to love Jesus and be indifferent about the church. * There's no such thing as an Individual follower of Christ. We must all belong to a body. 2. Spiritual leadership * To follow Jesus requires humility, gentleness, and willingness to take instructions from His word. * Jesus required the disciples to tarry together (Luke 24:49) * Faith in Jesus must also be accompanied by love for the saints. Three uncommon marks of a local church. 1. Proximity (Acts 2:46 ). God wants your fellowship to be close to your house as much as possible for accountability reasons. 2. Out-of-church fellowship. It is important that you have people in your space who share your faith. We all need spiritual support at some point in our lives. 3. Corporate prayer and corporate power. The power of God has a multiplier effect when we come together.

sermons.title: Training The Human Spirit
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: God’s design for His children is that every single one is a prophet. Psalms 105:13-15. Supernatural leading is not a one-man’s show. It is for all of God’s children. You won’t catch supernatural and prophetic signals if you don’t train your spirit. HOW TO TRAIN YOUR SPIRIT 1. Believe God is a Master Communicator. Exodus 3:2-4. 2. Start looking beyond your natural eyes. 1 Samuel 16:7 3. Prayer: Train your spirit by praying. Galatians 5:16-17. 4. Practice: If you don’t practice divine leading. It will die! Psalm 104:4 5. Consecration: Align your heart and life with the grand purpose of God which is the salvation of all mankind. 2 Cor. 5:19-21.

sermons.title: Soli Deo Gloria - PH Day 2 - Noon - Glorifying God With Our Bodies
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Glorifying God in your body What is Koinonia? The Greek word translated Koinonia as Communication which means Fellowship. Koinonia is fellowshipping together in the things of God because we have the same spirit. Koinonia demands that we hold each other accountable to the standard that Christ has set for us as Christians because our confession of faith places a demand on us (Philemon 1-6,16-17) We can fellowship in the things of God because we have the same spirit and by the fellowship, it’s easy for others to acknowledge that we’re from God. If you have Koinonia you'll love, you'll forgive, you'll put aside the sins of the flesh. Salvation creates in us a lifestyle that is consistent with the fruits of the spirit. The first teaching of overcoming temptation was done by God himself (Genesis 4:7-10, 3:15) The first sign of temptation is from your thoughts. Actions are the irresistible products of your thoughts. (James 1:14, 2Timothy 2:20)

sermons.title: Dating Code
sermons.speaker: Pst. Laju Iren
sermons.description: #LoveCode 1 Matthew 1:19 Every relationship begins with one person and that’s who you really are!! #LoveCode 2 Feelings are a scam col 3:2 Feelings are feeble therefore love gives chance to walk away. Relationships are not built on feelings and it’s important to know that it is a decision to make. #LoveCode 3 Love is a Choice..... you shouldn’t fix yourself I’m a relationship surrounded by circumstances. MISTAKES PEOPLE Make while choosing 1. Low Expectations 2. Unrealistic expectations: endeavor to look extremely beyond the present appearance!! 3. Wrong counsel: Getting counsels from the wrong hands make people chose wrong. #LoveCode 4 CHOOSE WITH GOD, you should build your life on principles and not expectations. Knowing that you are with your spouse for a purpose. #LoveCode 5 Dating is not a Hobby. #LoveCode 6 Marriage does not change people, do not ignore the Red Flags 🚩. #LoveCode 7 Love is Visionary Gen 28: Discern what God will have you do regarding your relationship. God should be involved all round. #LoveCode 8 Sex was God’s idea, waiting for God’s timing. Eph 5:3. A person who doesn’t understand fornicate won’t understand adultery. #LoveCode 9 Build Friendship, support, forgive and communicate. #LoveCode 10 Love is a fruit of the spirit. It is the fruit of your born again spirit. Therefore, your relationship with God is more important #LoveCode 11 Wisdom is a fruit of the spirit. #LoveCode 12 100% commitment but 100% sincerity #LoveCode 13 Successful Relationships are possible. Fix your thoughts into accepting that successful relationships are very possible. #LoveCode 14 GOD HAS A PLAN FOR YOUR RELATIONSHIP!!

sermons.title: Prophetic Faith
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Prophetic Faith When you look at the Eagles, many of the things people admire about them are natural tendencies for them. They do not 'strive' to soar, they simple do because it is their nature. That is the same way it should be with Christians. There are so many things that are already built-in us because we are in Christ, but yet we struggle with them. If we follow the fundamentals of faith like it's taught, we see some people who don't follow these fundamentals and yet they get a completely different result. Why then is the miraculous relatively easy for some people who don't seem to be following the 'principles' of faith. To understand God's mind concerning any subject at all, it is important that you pick every verse concerning the subject and interpret it as one. In Matthew 4:6, the devil quoted the scriptures to tempt Jesus and although the scripture was true, in that context, the motive was wrong. Faith has a context and faith has a motive. Mark 11:24 and James 4:3 shows that there should be a balance in the context and motive of Faith. Philippians 4:13 has been misinterpreted without understanding the context. The preceding verse explains the context for which that verse stands. The aim of faith is not self-gratification but God's glorification. What then is Faith? * Faith is the desire for the will of God, who has your best interest. * Faith is moving in God's direction and easing of heart. * Faith is not combating the will of God, but moving with God's passionate plan. Hebrews 11:33-40 * Faith is also the gift of the Spirit. There is a God-initiated Faith, where even when you are on your own, the Spirit is supplying Faith for a purpose. * Faith is both a gift of the Spirit and the fruit of the Spirit. The business of faith is to incline yourself to the heartbeat of God and following His pulse. There are things that God pursues with such fervency, and so if you align yourself with that you will walk in the prophetic faith. Loss reveals the purity of your faith. If it is faith, it is replaceable.

sermons.title: Bounce Back: The ecclesia advantage during crisis.
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: In 2 Corinthians 2:11, the Bible lets us know that we should not be ignorant of the devil's work by been discerning. Luke 22:31-32. The plan of the enemy is to attack you silently until you are finished. But when you become aware of his devices, what he planned to make you weak, would make you strong. The church needs to start creating space for people who have messed up and are remorseful. The church needs to be more forgiving. One of the things Satan does is for us to be unforgiving. God's strategy for the preservation of the saints is for us to strengthen one another. Galatians 6:1: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted. Romans 15:1-3 Salvation is NOT personal, we are to help one another in our moments of weakness. We MUST be responsible for one another. God is watching how we handle a brother who is in error.

sermons.title: RBC 25 - Raising an Unstoppable Prayer Movement
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Raising an Unstoppable Prayer Movement A word study on Israel: The name Israel is formed from two words — Sara and El. Sara means to contend, to wrestle, to fight. Hence, Yisrael means he who strives, contends, or wrestles with God. Israel became the name of an entire people because of one man’s encounter with God. In the New Testament, believers are called the Israel of God. Jesus is the real Israel — the true Son of God. Because of Him, we have become the Israel of God (Galatians 6:16). What makes us the Israel of God? 1. Faith in Jesus 2. A willingness to see the will of God manifest in your generation The free will of man confirms the sovereignty of God. God is in ultimate control, yet He invites us into partnership. We can cooperate with the Holy Spirit or hinder Him, and the sovereign God honours our choices. Many times, God makes things subject to our readiness. The sovereign God wants us to choose, and it requires determination and consistency. (Psalm 2:8).

sermons.title: The Working of The Spirit
sermons.speaker: Apostle Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: The Holy Spirit is given to every believer to guide, teach, and empower for godly living. The Spirit reveals the mind of God and brings understanding of spiritual truths. He works in the heart to produce fruit such as love, joy, peace, patience, and self-control. The Spirit empowers believers for service, ministry, and witness. Prayer is effective when led by the Spirit, who intercedes beyond human weakness. Walking in the Spirit means yielding daily to His leading rather than following the flesh. Spiritual gifts are distributed by the Spirit to build up the body of Christ. Sensitivity to the Spirit’s voice requires obedience, humility, and a pure heart. The Spirit convicts of sin, assures of salvation, and strengthens faith. The Christian life is victorious only through dependence on the Holy Spirit.

sermons.title: Triggers - How to Heal From Trauma
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: How to Heal from Trauma We must always embrace, in our consciousness, the image of totality in salvation. When it comes to total and wholesome deliverance, spiritual activity is the beginning and not the end. This means that mind renewal starts with the word of God and then a healthy exposure to the ideal reality. There are four attachment styles that influence people's behavior, which are 1. The Secure Attachment 2. ⁠The Anxious or Preoccupied 3. The ⁠Avoidant Dismissive 4. The Fearful Avoidant It is ideal to be conscious of what style we belong to so we can control or manage how we relate to people. There are, however, 3 sure ways to help heal from these negative effects: 1. The church. 2. Walking in the spirit of God. 3. Living in Christ Consciousness.

sermons.title: RC 23 - Ambience - Manifesting the Presence of God
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Ambience - Manifesting the presence of God.” 📍There are two ways by which God uses a man; 1. Stream of Faith 2. Stream of the Anointing Both work in a partnership. 📍Three things to note on the stream of faith: 1. Know the Word of God. 2. Believe the Word of God. 3. Practice the Word of God. 📍How do you stir up the anointing? 1. Through prayers. 2. By confessing the Word. 3. By responding to it! Never consider any signal ‘small’, always respond. 4. Heartfelt worship. The Word of God will have a sacred impact on the lives of sacred people—individuals who have learned to reverence the Word of God over any evidence to the contrary.

sermons.title: Deeper Uyo - Morning - Dimensions Of Building Intimacy With God
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Dimensions Of Building Intimacy With God" 🔥🔥🔥. It is one thing to be a Christian and it's another thing to grow. Being in church doesn't make you grow. God is calling you Deeper. Growth in God requires building intimacy with God. The 3 dimensions through which we can build intimacy with God includes: 1. His Names 2. His Son 3. His Spirit 📌 His Names: The name of God reveals the revelation of the specific reputation of God. You must experience God in entirety. You are doing yourself a disservice if you only experience the providential God without the God that heals, etc. 📌 His Son: In Jesus, you have the fullness of the Father. Develop a relationship with the Son, you cannot access God without the Son. (John 14:6) 📌 His Spirit: You cannot know the Son without the Spirit, it's in the Spirit that the Son benefits you. The born-again experience is impossible without the holy spirit. (Acts 2:33)

sermons.title: Live By Design - Special MDWK Service
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: One of the most important things you must know about purpose is that it is worship. We exist for his worship. The chief aim of man is to glorify God forever. Worship is not an option, it is a command. It is the purpose of your life. Psalm 147:1, Psalm 33:1 Psalm 150:6, Psalm 34:1 Hebrews 13:15 Philippians 2:5-10 The theology of worship is not predicated on how good you think God is. Acts 17:28 You depend on God and he created you that way. When God creates anything, he creates an atmosphere for them to survive. Genesis 1:11, 20 God didn’t create anything until the environment for which it will thrive was created. - [ ] What does it mean to be a son of God? 1. God is the source of your life. 2. You have the Holy Spirit Romans 8:15 3. You are God’s representative on the earth Genesis 6:2-3 There is a level of your purpose you cannot touch unless you have experienced his presence. In the presence is the intelligence for my assignment.

sermons.title: Special Sunday Service London Afternoon
sermons.speaker: Pst. Emmanuel Iren
sermons.description: Housing Spirits and Spiritual Gifts. Spiritual gifts are our way of describing the diverse ways that the same Spirit can operate. As a believer, you have the Holy Spirit dwelling in you and He can be expressed in different ways. The same Holy Spirit can work through you as the need demands. Don't limit Him! 1Chron 29:2, 4; 2Chron 7:1-2;6:18-20; Acts 7:47-49; Acts 17:24

sermons.title: Brighter
sermons.speaker: Pst. Laju Iren
sermons.description: Brighter ✨ In 2 kings 6:13-16, Elisha and his servant were in the same situation but they saw things differently. Elisha’s servant was afraid of what he could physically see while Elisha was not afraid because of his spiritual enlightenment. Your level of light is what determines your level of victory. Why do we need light? ✨Light in the word will keep you in difficult times ✨The Man in Christ is at an advantage when it comes to the word of God- 1 Corinthians 2:9-10. ✨The word of God cannot be difficult to understand because you have the Holy Spirit- 1 Corinthians 2:14 Points to Note 📝 Faith in Jesus Christ is the first step to understanding scriptures - Ephesians 1:15 📝There are dimensions of light and you determine your dimension Three dimensions of light ( The Parable of the Sower) 1.
